methods.description: Procedure for visiting study sites, collecting different types of algae and water samples, measuring water parameters in the field, and preserving/transporting samples.

steps.description: Within the scope of this research, the lakes in the Artabel Lakes Nature Park were visited two times.
inputs: Study site locations (Artabel Lakes Nature Park)
outputs: Visited sites (ARL, BL, ACL, YL, IL, YLP)

steps.description: Algae and water samples were taken from Artabel (ARL) and Beş (BL) Lakes. The second visit was made on 13 August 2016 and samples were taken from Acembol (ACL) and Yıldız (YL) Lakes, İsimsiz Lake (IL) and Yıldız Lakes Pond (YLP).
steps.searchable_summary: Collect algae and water samples from visited lakes

steps.description: Epipelic samples were collected employing a glass tube from a sediment surface at all the water bodies except Lake BL2 (Round, 1953;Sládečková 1962).
steps.searchable_summary: Collect epipelic algae samples from sediment surface using a glass tube

steps.description: Epilithic samples were taken from ARL1, ARL2, BL2, ACL1, ACL2, ACL3, and IL lakes. Randomly chosen stones were scraped with a tooth brush and then washed into plastic bottles.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect epilithic algae samples by scraping stones with a toothbrush

steps.description: For epiphytic samples, mosses (Hygrohypnum luridum (Hedwig) Jennings (1913:287)) and filamentous alga (Microspora Thuret 1850) were taken from ARL1, ARL3, YL1, YL2, YL3, ACL2, ACL3, IL lakes, and YLP Pond. Filamentous green algae such as Microspora, Oedogonium, and Ulothrix were taken by hand.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect epiphytic algae samples from mosses and filamentous algae by hand

steps.description: All samples were fixed in a solution of 4 % neutral formaldehyde.
steps.searchable_summary: Preserve collected samples using 4% neutral formaldehyde solution

steps.description: In the field, water temperature, pH, dissolved oxygen, total dissolved solids (TDS), and electrical conductivity were measured using an Orion4Star and YSI 55 portable measuring instruments at each sampling station.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure water quality parameters (temperature, pH, DO, TDS, conductivity) in the field using portable instruments
outputs: Water parameter measurements (temperature, pH, dissolved oxygen, TDS, electrical conductivity)

steps.description: Samples were transported to the laboratory in the icebox.
steps.searchable_summary: Transport samples to the laboratory in an icebox

methods.description: Procedures for examining, identifying, photographing, and analyzing collected algal samples and related data in the laboratory.

steps.description: In the laboratory, samples were examined in temporary slides and under a Leica DM 2500 model light microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: Examine samples under a light microscope using temporary slides

steps.description: Algae were photographed with camera Leica DFC 290 attached to the microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: Photograph observed algae using a microscope-attached camera

steps.description: All of the taxa were identified according to West & West (1904, 1905, 1908, 1912, 1923, Prescott (1962), Ruzicka (1977), Lind & Brook (1980), Huber-Pestalozzi (1982), Dillard (1990, 1991, 1993), Bourrelly & Coute (1991), Gábor (1995), Lenzenweger (1996, 1997, 1999), John et al. (2003), Komárek & Anagnostidis (1998, Wotowski & Hindak (2005), Hindak (2008)
steps.searchable_summary: Identify algal taxa based on microscopic observations, photographs, and comparison with taxonomic literature
inputs: Taxonomic literature (West & West, Prescott, Ruzicka, etc.)

steps.description: Calculation of similarity was doing in the GRAPHS program (Novakovsky 2004)
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate similarity between samples or sites using the GRAPHS program

steps.description: biological and environmental data relationships analysis were done in the Statistica 12.0 and CANOCO (Ter Braak & Šmilauer 2002) programs.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze relationships between biological and environmental data using Statistica and CANOCO software

steps.description: Bio indication analysis was done with the help of ecological preferences of revealed desmid algae species (Barinova et al. 2006).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform bioindication analysis using ecological preferences of identified species

steps.description: Frequencies of algal taxa were determined according to the following scale based on the number of lakes studied in Artabel Lakes Nature Park. Very rare (1): taxa recorded in 1-20 % of investigated lakes; rare (2): in 21-40 %; common (3): in 41-60 %; frequent (4): in 61-80 %; and very frequent (5): in 81-100 % of investigated lakes (Kocataş 1992).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate frequency of algal taxa occurrence across lakes based on a defined percentage scale
inputs: Occurrence data per lake for each taxon
